title: Kasparov vs Topalov
intro: In this game, watch for how the control of squares near the kings evolves, as well as the creation and removal of hanging pieces. The tactical opportunities arise from captures and checks that can shift the balance of power.
summary: The pressure around the kings evolved with White's king moving to c1, increasing threats in that area. Black responded by repositioning the rook, creating a hanging pawn on c3. White's queen then moved to a7, expanding its influence and threatening several squares.

Black plays d6, attacking five new squares and solidifying control.
